title: Buttondown vs Campaign Monitor (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Buttondown vs Campaign Monitor: compare pricing, features, and real use cases to find the right email tool for your needs.
h1: Buttondown vs Campaign Monitor: Side-by-Side Comparison (2025)
intro: Choosing between Buttondown and Campaign Monitor comes down to your specific use case. Both are legitimate email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. Buttondown is built for developers and technical writers who prefer markdown, while Campaign Monitor targets agencies and teams managing multiple brands. This comparison covers the key differences so you can decide quickly.

comparison_table.tool_a: Developers And Technical Writers Who Prefer Markdown
comparison_table.tool_b: Agencies And Teams Managing Multiple Brands

choose_a_if: You are primarily focused on developers and technical writers who prefer markdown
choose_b_if: You are primarily focused on agencies and teams managing multiple brands

faq.q: Is Buttondown better than Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: It depends on your use case. Buttondown wins for developers and technical writers who prefer markdown, while Campaign Monitor is stronger for agencies and teams managing multiple brands. Neither is objectively better — the right choice depends on your specific needs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Buttondown or Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Buttondown starts at a lower price point ($9/mo vs $11/mo). However, pricing scales with subscriber count, so the total cost depends on your list size.

faq.q: Can I switch from Campaign Monitor to Buttondown?
faq.a: Yes, switching from Campaign Monitor to Buttondown is possible. Most platforms support CSV import/export for subscriber lists. Plan for some time to recreate automations and templates, as these rarely transfer directly between platforms.
